HAPPENING: Fertilizer Shortage Causes Supply Value to 2x

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-04-30 06:49 UTC

OP

The Iran war is setting up a shortage of urea fertilizer, which is already driving up prices, and will disrupt the food supply, sooner or later.

The world fertilizer market is worth, roughly, 290 Billion UDS according to fertilizer.com, and 30% of the world supply moves through, and is currently blocked within the straight of Hormuz. In the past month, the price has doubled, from roughly $400 to $720. Which means that the big money is yet to be made, we are Early.

Anons could speculate in high Nitrogen fertilizer and profit from the misery and poverty of the 3rd world to line our pockets, and laugh in our mustaches and cigar smoke

Neeting and waging are two sides of the same coin. Balance in all things and you will find peace.

Those who seek the light have experienced immense darkness. Brother, this is a moment of rejoice. Accept your pain, let it go, move on and forgive yourself or the people who have cause it. But remember brother the world is simply an illusion and by that I mean your own perception of reality. It's all inside you and not in the outside world. A river is not beautiful or magnificent, that's your illusion of it. A river is simply water that is moving, nothing more, nothing less. The illusion feels real because just like when you dream the pain feels real, the fear feels real, the sorrow feels real. Once you wake up from the dream you realize that nothing was real, that it was just an illusion. But it is true that when you were in the dream the pain felt real. So, once we see the illusion for what it is it'll start dissolving and once it dissolves that's then the reason to work inside the world without attachments and to help those who are asleep to wake up.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Once all of those dissolve it'll uncover enlightenment. So, enlightenment isn't an achievement or something you do, it's literally that you stop doing. It's the fact that the doer stops. The watcher and the watched go away. The only thing that remains is the watching.

They intellectualize, "There may be hope for other people, but there's no hope for me.". Such a potent intellectual defense mechanism. And what are they defending against? The belief of hope. Because hope hurts. If there's zero chance, there's zero need for effort. See, human beings are really resilient. We can live through genocide and holocaust and all kinds of stuff. We can live through all kinds of terrible things, terrible economic times. We're survivors. But don't give us hope, because if we can hope, we can hurt.

Having hope also exposes you to hurt. And hurt is a normal part of life. If you're hurting that means you're human. It's just. Are you going to hurt consciously and change something or are you going to hurt unconsciously and keep on hurting due to the same thing for the rest of your life?

You maybe haven't "You go this" at the moment. You may feel powerless. You may feel down. And at the same time you'll slowly and gradually regain your power. You'll unburden the weight. Sooner or later you'll feel so light you'll start flying. I really believe that, even though it may at the current moment not feel like it. Even though you might feel like the current moment is never ending trust me, it'll all change and reshape into a healthier future. You got this fren who's reading this.

Your dear Vishnu Kumar wishes you are peaceful day. May Vishnu of the mindchain be with and all around you.

Capex into datacenters are still going full steam, construction is busier than ever, many of them won't get finished but a lot more will. Power bills go up, restructuring continues meaning even more people vying for any job still available and when it turns around the economy restructuring to productive things will take years. We are also entering a the final 30-45 day window before 500 million to 1 billion starve to death or get real close to it. Haber Bosch is partially shut down and even if it comes back on tomorrow farmers are already getting fucked and avoiding planting if not going under entirely. Much of the unrest and death will be in the third world or at least it will start there. Hard mode hasn't even begun yet.

What would you even go to besides proof of work? To me, its like the diesel engine of consensus algos. It might be a bit dirty and sometimes clunky, but it just works (until you add infinity layers of extra bullshit to clog up the gears).
I don't think crypto has a backend issue on the whole anymore, adoption and real use has gone down since a decade ago (I humbly and regrettably spent most of my btc in the mid 2010s on whatever I could buy with it to try and do my small part in getting its notoriety and utility as a currency up). I think the speculation mania of 2017 still lingers like a spectre over the whole ecosystem that disincentivizes spending, because why spend what amounts to a lottery ticket in your account when it could 10x by next week? If some smart contract payment system got executed on a big name chain that devised a payment schedule such as the following:
>see bing bing wahoo on store
>pay in eth (or whatever)
>month passes, get bing bing wahoo
>eth price action explodes
>get smart contract executed arbitrage refund for *part* of the difference in fiat valuation (let's say 75%)
>vendor still profits
>buyer gets good/service, doesn't feel completely fleeced by bad timing
Something like that could make ecommerce and crypto volume explode, and I say that as somebody that thinks every single smart contract platform is cool in theory but absolute ass on execution.
BTW xmr is also my favorite token that I have exactly zero balance for the same reason lmao. Its my long term QQQ/VOO when I'm able to put real money aside for it
